headline: The network of US pilots helping women seek abortion care
description: Responding to increasingly strict anti-abortion laws in Texas, a non-profit organization offers free flights to neighboring states. The group also assists individuals seeking gender-affirming care.

headline: 'It was 30°C in the classrooms this afternoon, it was very tough': France experiences its hottest day in May on record
Israeli strikes kill at least 31 in south Lebanon as Israel expands ground operations
Iran says ready to retaliate after overnight US strikes target Iranian missile sites and boats
description: American forces conducted 'self-defense strikes' in southern Iran 'to protect our troops from threats posed by Iranian forces,' a US Central Command spokesman said of the latest attacks, which threaten an already fragile ceasefire that began April 8 as the United States and Iran struggle to reach an accord to end a war.

headline: Sweden's Greta Thunberg and her fellow climate activists balance hope and despair
description: For seven years, filmmaker Helena Molin followed a dozen teenagers who helped found the Fridays for Future movement in 2018. The resulting documentary has been met with critical acclaim.

headline: Roland-Garros 2026: Loïs Boisson's comeback after long injury ends in early exit
description: Defeated in straight sets (2-6, 2-6) by Russia's Anna Kalinskaya in the opening round, the French player, in far from her best form, could not recapture the magic that had carried her to the semi-finals of the 2025 tournament.

headline: 'Believing they already serve humanity, Musk, Bezos and Thiel see no need for philanthropy'
description: With his foundation, Bill Gates embodied philanthropy. But his ties to Jeffrey Epstein have tarnished his reputation, and the new tech tycoons now place more faith in themselves than in governments to improve humanity, writes Le Monde's Arnaud Leparmentier in his column.

French airlines are finally turning to sustainable fuels to break free from fossil fuel dependence
How China uses influencers to polish its image
description: Chongqing, a futuristic Chinese city, has caught the eye of influencers and bloggers. Beijing, meanwhile, welcomes new visitors, often unaware of the geopolitical and ideological currents running beneath their social media posts.

headline: A €100,000 contract and possible mistaken identity: New clues emerge in murder of anti-drug activist's brother
description: The case file shows that the Marseille murder stemmed from major organized crime, involving several competing teams of hitmen. According to some suspects' statements, it was Amine Kessaci and not his brother Mehdi who was targeted, due to his commitment to fighting drug trafficking.
byline: Grégoire Biseau,  Jérémie Pham-Lê and  Lorraine de Foucher

headline: The post-May '68 Christian philosophy now being embraced by the American far right
description: Trump supporters often blame the cultural upheavals triggered by the May 1968 protests in France for sowing the seeds of 'wokeness.' Yet some of them are now claiming a different French school of thought, which took shape at Stanford University under the influence of René Girard.
